hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume

branch_design_rationale.why_old_basin_is_not_enough: the old post-unify family only moved max within the 99.6x band, so it is not a credible route to max<50
branch_design_rationale.new_hypothesis: Ka2_y remains saturated because late excitation is too concentrated locally; distributing the same legal sign-separated late-block cycle across anchors 9/10/11 may create qualitatively stronger observability
branch_design_rationale.mechanism_safety_rule: every insertion is a closed loop at a real reachable anchor state and must reconnect exactly before the next base action

candidate_specs.name: redistribute_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Leave the old local basin and place the same full continuity-safe late-block cycle at both anchors 10 and 11. Theory: repeated sign-separated late Y exposure at two distinct late z-family faces may attack the Ka2_y ceiling more structurally than any single-anchor dose tweak.

candidate_specs.name: redistribute_l10_l11_fullblock_y10x2
candidate_specs.rationale: Same distributed double-block idea, but use the stronger mean-oriented full block at both 10 and 11 to test whether multi-anchor redistribution can keep the structure stable while attacking Ka2_y more strongly.

candidate_specs.name: redistribute_l9_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Push the structural idea further: distribute the full late-block cycle across anchors 9, 10, and 11 so the weak late segment is excited at three consecutive z-family base states rather than one local pocket.

candidate_specs.name: redistribute_l9_l10_l11_fullblock_y6x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Lighter triple-block sibling. Keeps the same three-anchor redistribution but reduces each full-block Y dwell to see whether the ceiling move comes from distribution itself rather than raw dwell size.

candidate_specs.name: redistribute_l9_ypair_neg4_plus_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Add a dedicated anchor9 inner-y priming loop before the double full-block redistribution on 10/11. Theory: if Ka2_y remains weak because the late segment enters the distributed block with insufficient y-preconditioning, this branch should help.

candidate_specs.name: redistribute_l9_zpair_neg6_plus_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Alternative anchor9 preconditioning using a strong z-pair instead of a y-pair. This tests whether the redistributed late blocks need a cleaner z-family entrance rather than more local y-dose.

bottom_line.branch_verdict: best new distributed branch `redistribute_l9_ypair_neg4_plus_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0` changed mean/max by +0.091 / -2.732 versus the unified winner; the strongest max-oriented distributed branch `redistribute_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0` changed them by -0.003 / -0.561.
bottom_line.sub50_plausibility: not plausible from current evidence: even after leaving the old basin, the best landed max is still 100.194, far from 50.

scientific_conclusion: This structural redistribution test answered the new question directly. Repeating full continuity-safe late-block cycles across anchors 9/10/11 did create a genuinely different valid family, but the landed batch still reached only max=100.194 at best. That means the Ka2_y ceiling is not just a small local-dose problem at 10/11; under the current hard constraints and this distributed closed-loop mechanism-safe design space, the evidence still says sub-50 is not realistically within reach.
